mappings.rationale: Directly covers social differentiation, stratification, class systems, and social mobility.
Directly covers upper class, elites, and status-based stratification as forms of social hierarchy (subsections B.1 and B.3).
Covers Marxist theories of social class, class relationships, and the idea of a classless society (subsections B.2 and B.5).
Covers caste systems, age and sex differentiation, and comparative approaches to social stratification.
Covers racial differentiation as a factor producing social and economic differences (subsection A.2).
Covers sex differentiation as a factor in social stratification and challenges to gender-based hierarchies (subsection A.2).
Covers philosophical analysis of sex-based social differentiation and its role in stratification.
Covers the historical role of gender in social differentiation and stratification.
Covers economic differentiation as the basis of class stratification in capitalist societies (subsection A.3.a).
Covers the communist pursuit of a classless society and alternative approaches to stratification (subsection B.5).
Covers socialist theories of class relationships and visions of equality (subsections B.2 and B.5).
Covers Tocqueville's analysis of democratic equality, social mobility, and class structure in America.
Covers slavery as an extreme form of social stratification and its effects on status and mobility.
Covers the movement to end slavery as a challenge to racial-based social stratification.
Covers how democratic governance relates to class, status, and power as forms of stratification (subsection A.3.b).
Covers economic inequality at the bottom of the stratification system, relevant to class differentiation.
Covers the psychological effects of differentiation and stratification on individuals (subsection A.3.c).
Covers the Hindu caste system as a major example of social stratification (subsection B.1).
Covers challenges to racial stratification and the pursuit of social mobility and equality.
Covers how social identity intersects with class, status, and power in stratified societies.
Covers analytical frameworks for studying social differentiation and stratification.
Covers how ideological frameworks justify or challenge systems of social stratification.
Covers how economic development has shaped patterns of wealth distribution and class formation across societies.
